comment: `AutonomousAgent` is SUMO's direct, explicitly-defined bearer class for "something or someone that can act on its own and produce changes in the world," refined into sentient/cognitive/AI-agent subtypes. Confirmed `(subclass AutonomousAgent Object)` in `Merge.kif` -- SUMO models this at the object layer, same pattern as DOLCE and YAMATO.

GUM's own comment defines `ConsciousBeing` as "an active entity capable of producing information that may be ascribed consciousness; e.g. a person" -- the clearest direct anchor for this candidate among all eight sources, verified verbatim against the fetched `GUM-31.owl.txt`.

Confirmed directly from Fig. 2's diagram (not extractable as text -- verified by rendering the page as an image): `weak agent` is a direct child of `continuant > object`, alongside `morphological whole`, sibling to the `functional` branch (`living organism`, `artifact`). It is a real object-side agentive label, but the paper's own body text never elaborates it beyond the figure, so `overlap` rather than `close-match`.

D18 section 3.4.2 confirmed verbatim: "a special place have those to which we ascribe intentions, beliefs, and desires. These are called Agentive" (physical layer), plus a separate Social Agent layer including legal persons and contractants (Table 1: "a (legal) person, a contractant"). Agentivity is explicitly modeled but split across the object/social-object layers rather than reified as one bearer class, so `overlap` rather than `close-match`.
Confirmed verbatim: "A type of conscious being pronominalizeable by `she`, `he`, etc. rather than by `it`." A genuine conscious-being subtype, but peripheral to the core scope, not a peer anchor.

Confirmed verbatim: both are subclasses of `Person` defined only "for, e.g., pronominalization purposes." Pronominalization refinements, not core bearer-class content -- equivalence should not be asserted at the concept level.

subject_id: GUM:Believe / Cognition / Know / Perception / Think / Wanting / MentalActive / MentalInactive / Disliking / Fearing / Liking / ReactionAndEmotion / Striving
comment: Confirmed verbatim for every term: each is commented as a "mental process" or "mental reaction" (e.g. `Think`: "The mental process of thinking"; `ReactionAndEmotion`: "An inactive mental process..."), all descending from `Internal` (a process branch), not from any bearer/entity class. These belong with `process.md`/`event.md`, not here.

Confirmed directly from Fig. 2's diagram: `mind` is a direct child of `semi-abstract` (sibling to `content` and `representation`), not of `continuant`/`object`. YAMATO's own text defines semi-abstract things as needing "only time to exist" -- the opposite ontological commitment from a bearer/agent class.
No agent/mind/conscious-being term of any kind found in `bfo-core.ttl` (direct grep against the fetched file, 2026-07-06).

The 2021 paper does not introduce a dedicated Agent/Mind/ConsciousBeing class -- confirmed by a full-text search of the freshly re-extracted paper, which finds "agent" only inside an unrelated bibliography title. `Person` does appear repeatedly as `ObjectKind(Person)` in the paper's own worked OntoUML examples (Teacher/Student, Jogger, Walker, Spouse), but per this repository's established precedent for this same source (`process.md`'s "Jogging Process" finding), a worked example instantiation is not the same as a formally introduced core-theory class -- so this stays `unknown` rather than becoming a positive correspondence.

No reified `Agent`/`Person`/`Mind` class found in `modules/gfo-base.owl`, but the module is not silent on the topic: it declares `Mental_stratum`, `Awareness_level`, `Personality_level`, and `Social_stratum` classes, and `Social_stratum`'s own definition states "Agents are the bearers of the social roles that humans play" -- a conceptual acknowledgment of agents without a corresponding bearer-class declaration in this module. Kept at `unknown` rather than a blank absence because the distinction matters: this material is directly relevant to the separate "Ontological Level / Stratum" candidate later in the `0.3.0` batch.
No agent/mind/conscious-being term found in the fetched `tupper.all.owl` or `tupper.clif` (direct grep, excluding the `MinDim` false-positive substring match on "mind").
